rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FAY JEAN CHIN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. The successor(s)-in-interest to post-deceased beneficiary Helen Fong has/have not yet been determined. Nothing has been filed to confirm that Helen Fong's estate was ordered to be distributed to her three children. The correct I&A should be FILED now. Status of filing a petition for final distribution or another report of status with a petition requesting additional time to administer the estate must be filed by 11/3/26. Appearance will be required on 11/17/26 if neither petition is filed. (Issues raised by the court should be addressed in a supplement or amendment that stands on its own and simply provides the missing or correct information, not in response to examiner's notes, which are not part of the record.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/4/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The I&A filed on 9/10/25 shows the real property on Attachment 1. A personal representative may only appraise cash and cash equivalents. A probate referee must appraise all other assets. A Corrected I&A must be filed, showing an appraisal by the probate referee. (The probate referee appointed on 6/27/24 has retired. The administrator must file the local form Request for Appointment of Probate Referee and submit an endorsed filed copy of that with the Order Appointing Probate Referee.) (4) There was reportedly also a bank account, but that was not reported on the I&A. (5) If, however, there were no assets in the estate, because the real property was transferred to the surviving joint tenants by operation of law, and the surviving heirs could have claimed the small bank account via a 13100 affidavit, the correct petition to close estate administration would have been one pursuant to Probate Code Section 12251. (6) The proposed order that was lodged is not a proper order for "final distribution" and does not reflect what was in the prayer. Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/21/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If the administrator distributed the cash without a prior order, at the very least, he must file a receipt by the other heir along with verification of the balance in the account on the date of death or with the other heir's waiver of account.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]
